questions.question_text: What is the electronic configuration for the particle^27_13Al+?

The data in the table gives the 5th to the 10th ionisation energies of three elements from Period 3 of the Periodic Table. ionisation energy/kJmol^–1 element 5th 6th 7th 8th 9th 10th X 6274 21269 25398 29855 35868 40960 Y 7012 8496 27107 31671 36579 43140 Z 6542 9362 11018 33606 38601 43963 What are the correct identities of these three elements? element X element Y element Z
What contains 9.03  10^23 oxygen atoms?

Methane and steam react to produce hydrogen. CH_4(g)  +  2H_2O(g)    CO_2(g)  +  4H_2(g) 0.80g of methane and 1.35g of steam react. One of the reactants is used up. Which volume of hydrogen, measured at room conditions, will be produced?
Which statement explains why sodium and potassium have different melting points?

options.A: The attraction between cations and delocalised electrons is stronger in sodium.
options.B: The attraction between cations and anions is stronger in sodium.
options.C: The attraction between atoms is stronger in sodium.
options.D: The attraction between nuclei and shared electron pairs is stronger in sodium.

questions.question_text: NH_3 and HCN react together to form NH_4CN, an ionic compound. Which row states the number of coordinate bonds and the number of  bonds in one formula unit of NH_4CN? number of number of coordinate bonds  bonds
When 1.0aqueous sodium hydroxide, 55mol of ethanoic acid, CHkJmol^3COOH, in aqueous solution is neutralised by an excess of _–1 of energy is released. Which statement about this reaction is correct?

options.A: The reaction is exothermic because only bond breaking takes place.
options.B: The reaction is exothermic because only bond forming takes place.
options.C: The reaction is exothermic because more energy is given out in breaking bonds than is taken in to form bonds.
options.D: The reaction is exothermic because more energy is given out in forming bonds than is taken in to break bonds.

questions.question_text: In an experiment, 1.60g of a fuel is burnt. 45.0% of the energy released is absorbed by 200g of water. The temperature of the water rises from 18.0°C to 66.0°C. What is the total energy released per gram of fuel burnt?

Sulfite ions, SO_3^2–, react separately with zinc and with manganese dioxide. a, b, c, d, w, x, y and z are all whole numbers. aSO_3^2–(aq)  +  bH_2O(l)  +  Zn(s)    Zn^2+(aq)  +  cOH^–(aq)  +  dS_2O_4^2–(aq) MnO_2(s)  +  wSO_3^2–(aq)  +  xH^+(aq)    Mn^2+(aq)  +  yS_2O_6^2–(aq)  +  zH_2O(l) Which numbers are correct for a, b, w and x? a b w x
Which equation shows hydrogen acting as an oxidising agent?
g of nitrogen gas is stored in a 2.00dm^3 vessel at 40.0°C. What is the pressure in the vessel?

One molecule of haemoglobin, Hb, can bind with four molecules of oxygen according to the equation shown. Hb(aq)  +  4O_2(aq)    Hb(O_2)_4(aq) When the equilibrium concentration of O_2 is 7.6  10^–6moldm^–3, the equilibrium concentrations of Hb and Hb(O_2)_4 are equal. What is the numerical value of K_c  for this equilibrium?

Aqueous acid P and aqueous alkali Q have the same concentration. 20cm^3 of P is added to a conical flask. Q is slowly added to the flask and the volume of Q and the pH are recorded. The pH titration curve is shown. 14 12 10 pH86 4 2 0 02468 10 12 1416 18 20 22 24 26 28 30 volume of Q added/cm3 Which row gives the identity of P and Q? P Q

Photochromic glass, used for sunglasses, darkens when exposed to bright light and becomes more transparent again when the light is less bright. The darkness of the glass is due to the presence of silver atoms. The following reactions are involved. reaction 1 Ag^+  +  Cl^–  Ag  +  Cl reaction 2 Cu^+  +  Cl  Cu^2+  +  Cl^– reaction 3 Cu^2+  +  Ag    Cu^+  +  Ag^+ Which statement about these reactions is correct?

options.A: Cu^+ and Cu^2+ ions act as catalysts.
options.B: Cu^+ ions act as an oxidising agent in reaction 2.
options.C: Reaction 3 increases the darkness of the glass.
options.D: Silver atoms are reduced in reaction 3.

questions.question_text: The reversible reaction between methanol and ethanoic acid liquids is catalysed by adding a small volume of concentrated sulfuric acid. Two statements about this reaction are listed. 1 The sulfuric acid is a homogeneous catalyst. 2 The sulfuric acid lowers the activation energy of the reverse reaction. Which statements are correct?

The atomic radii and ionic radii for three elements in Period 3 are shown. atomic radius/nm ionic radius/nm element X 0.118 0.053 element Y 0.099 0.180 element Z 0.160 0.072 Using this data, which statement is correct?

options.A: Element X has lower electrical conductivity than element Y.
options.B: Element Y has a higher melting point than element X.
options.C: Element Y and element Z react to form an ionic compound.
options.D: Element Z forms ionic compounds by gaining electrons.

questions.question_text: Three equations are listed. x, y and z are all whole numbers. 1 xAl  +  yO_2zAl_2O_3 2 xMg  +  yO_2zMgO 3 xNa  +  yO_2zNa_2O Which equations can be balanced if x = 4 and z = 2?
Which oxide has a simple structure rather than a giant structure?
The trends seen in Group 2 can be used to predict the properties of radium and its compounds. Which statement is correct?

options.A: Radium has the highest second ionisation energy of the elements in Group 2.
options.B: Radium hydroxide is the least soluble of the hydroxides of the elements in Group 2.
options.C: Radium carbonate has the lowest thermal stability of the carbonates of the elements in Group 2.
options.D: Radium reacts faster with water than the other elements in Group 2.

questions.question_text: Equal masses of CaCO_3, Ca(NO_3)_2, BaCO_3 and Ba(NO_3)_2 are thermally decomposed. The volume of gas produced in each experiment is measured under the same conditions. Which compound will produce the greatest volume of gas?

Equation 1 and equation 2 show two different reactions of halide ion Q^– with concentrated sulfuric acid. equation 1 H_2SO_4  +  Q^–  HSO_4^–  +  HQ equation 2 H_2SO_4  +  2Q^–  +  2H^+  SO_2  +  Q_2  +  2H_2O What is Q?
What happens when iodine solution is added to a solution of sodium bromide?

options.A: A reaction occurs without changes in oxidation state.
options.B: Bromide ions are oxidised; iodine atoms are reduced.
options.C: Bromide ions are reduced; iodine atoms are oxidised.

questions.question_text: Which statement is correct?

options.A: Nitrogen is unreactive due to the absence of lone pairs in the molecule.
options.B: Aqueous ammonia contains both NH_3(aq) and NH_4^+(aq). The NH_4^+(aq) ion is a Brønsted–Lowry acid.
options.C: High temperatures are needed to supply the energy to break the strong double bonds in nitrogen molecules when they react.
options.D: Atmospheric SO_2 reacts with unburnt hydrocarbons to form a component of photochemical smog.

questions.question_text: When dry ammonia and hydrogen chloride gases are mixed, a solid white ionic compound is formed. Two statements are listed. 1 The formation of the ionic compound is a redox reaction. 2 During the formation of the ionic compound, the H–N–H bond angle increases. Which statements are correct?
The diagrams show skeletal formulas of some isomers of C_6H_12O. O O 12 OO 34 Which statement is correct?

options.A: 1 and 3 are chain isomers of each other and positional isomers of each other.
options.B: 2 and 3 are functional group isomers of each other and both have a chiral centre.
options.C: 1 and 4 are functional group isomers of each other and both have a chiral centre.
options.D: 2 and 4 are positional isomers of each other and functional group isomers of each other.

questions.question_text: The skeletal formulas of two compounds are shown. progesteronetestosterone O OH OO Which statements about progesterone and testosterone are correct?

options.A: They both contain a ketone group, and they both have geometrical isomers due to the C=C bond.
options.B: They have the same molecular formula, and they both have geometrical isomers.
options.C: They have the same number of chiral carbons, and they have the same molecular formula.
options.D: They have the same number of chiral carbons, and they both contain a ketone group.

questions.question_text: Which intermediate ion forms in the greatest amount during the addition of HBr to propene?

Two hydrocarbons, R–CH_3 and R'–CH_3, react separately with bromine in the presence of ultraviolet light. One of these hydrocarbons is unsaturated. In each case, free radical substitution reactions occur. R is CH_3CHC(CH_3). R' is CH_3CH(CH_3)CH_2. Which row is correct? a propagation stage for a termination stage for the saturated hydrocarbon the unsaturated hydrocarbon

options.A: R–CH_2•  +  Br•    R–CH_2Br R'–CH_2•  +  Br•    R'–CH_2Br
options.B: R–CH_2•  +  Br_2  R–CH_2Br  +  Br• R'–CH_2•  +  Br_2  R'–CH_2Br  +  Br•

questions.question_text: The fumes from the exhausts of petrol-burning cars contain the following pollutants. 1 unburnt hydrocarbons 2 nitrogen dioxide 3 carbon monoxide Which pollutants are removed by oxidation in a catalytic converter?

Compound X, C_7H_11ICl_2, is dissolved in ethanol and the solution mixed with warm aqueous silver nitrate. A precipitate is seen immediately. compound X CH_2Cl Cl I What is the colour of this precipitate and what is the structural formula of the first organic product? colour of structural formula of the precipitate the first organic product CH_2Cl HO
,4-dibromobutane reacts with an excess of ethanolic sodium hydroxide until no further reaction takes place. What is the relative formula mass of the major organic product?
A small section of a polymer produced from two monomers is shown. –CH_2–C(CH_3)_2–CH(CH_3)–CH_2– What are the two monomers?
The balanced equation for the reaction of X with an excess of acidified K_2Cr_2O_7 is shown. X  +  3[O]    Y  +  H_2O Compound Y is the only organic product of the reaction. Which compound is X? A
Three mixtures are heated under reflux. Which mixtures will produce sodium propanoate as one product? 1 CH_3CH_2CHO  +  NaBH_4(aq) 2 CH_3CH_2CH_2OH  +  Na(s) 3 CH_3CH_2CN  +  NaOH(aq)
Which compound is a product of the hydrolysis of CH_3CO_2CH_2C_2H_5 using aqueous sodium hydroxide?
Compound Q reacts when heated with HCN in the presence of a catalyst to produce compound R. Molecules of compound R each contain four carbon atoms. What is compound Q? AB

The table shows the reagents and products of three reactions. reagents products 1 ethanoic acid + Na_2CO_3CH_3CO_2Na  +  H_2O  +  CO_2 2 ethanoic acid + Mg (CH_3CO_2)_2Mg  +  H_2O 3 ethanoic acid + CH_3OH CH_3CO_2CH_3  +  H_2O Which rows are correct?

Seven aldehydes and ketones ● contain only one oxygen atom per molecule ● have four or fewer carbon atoms in their structures. Alkaline I_2(aq) is added to each of these carbonyl compounds separately. How many of these carbonyl compounds produce a yellow precipitate with alkaline I_2(aq)?

Some properties of compound X are listed. ● 1.0mol X reacts with exactly 8.5mol oxygen when completely combusted. ● X does not react when heated with acidified KMnO_4. What are the possible identities of X? OHHOO OH 1234

A sample of magnesium contains the isotopes ^24Mg, ^25Mg and ^26Mg only. The percentage abundance of ^25Mg and ^26Mg is the same. The relative atomic mass of magnesium in the sample is 24.3. What is the percentage abundance of ^24Mg?
